scope.selection: sources present in the user-provided public list but absent from the 188-source broad discovery capture

findings: The repository is a two-file ranked list with direct installation commands, not a Skill payload source.

Its recommendations mix official, third-party, service-connected, suite, index, and tooling entries; recommendation tiers and star thresholds are discovery opinions, not safety, quality, superiority, license, or admission proof.
Every child repository and every install instruction requires independent source pinning and review.
sources.nextGate: deduplicate its child sources against the broad pool and select only marginal candidates tied to a concrete shortfall

findings: Existing review already split design guidance, brand-asset provenance, HTML/media export, voiceover, and bundled assets rather than treating the repository as one safe payload.
The current revision has material drift, so the prior not-approved review cannot establish current-body safety, quality, or portability.
No Hook was detected, but browser, image, audio, export, dependency, credential, copyright, and output-consent boundaries remain.
sources.nextGate: review the 14 changed files against the prior component and asset boundaries only if a design shortfall selects this source

findings: The body promotes explicit assumptions, simplicity, surgical changes, and goal-driven verification with a stated caution-versus-speed tradeoff.
These principles substantially overlap current intake, minimal-sufficiency, dirty-work preservation, disciplined coding, review, diagnose, and closure behavior.
No executable or Hook surface was detected, but source attribution and redistribution remain incomplete without a root license artifact.
sources.nextGate: advance only if a concrete coding-behavior shortfall survives current-chain comparison and license provenance is resolved

findings: Existing review already requires suite decomposition across shipping, analytics, execution, market, discovery, strategy, growth, toolkit, legal, privacy, data, SQL, and script boundaries.
The current release changes 27 files, including evidence-oriented shipping audits, so the prior not-approved component review must not be silently promoted to the current revision.
Source breadth does not justify whole-suite admission; overlap and data, account, web, legal, privacy, script, and side-effect boundaries remain component-specific.
sources.nextGate: select a concrete PM shortfall and compare one atomic component or composition against current official, runtime, and curated alternatives

findings: This is primarily an operational CLI and public discovery surface, not a source of end-user capability bodies for direct admission.

It overlaps CC Switch management functions and the retired custom Manager experiment, while adding temporary use, interactive Agent launch, telemetry, source parsing, installation, update, removal, and multi-Agent path writes.
Its 84 executable files and setup-changing commands require a separate tooling review; no command was run and no runtime or Agent path was changed.
sources.nextGate: consult only for dated interoperability evidence or a concrete CC Switch capability gap; do not revive the custom Manager

batchDecision.nextMarginalBatch: deduplicate child sources from the awesome list, refresh only demand-selected Huashu or PM component deltas, and keep Vercel CLI separate from Skill admission

recheckTriggers: a source revision, license artifact, suite structure, executable surface, Hook surface, or maintainer state changes
a concrete shortfall selects a child source or one Huashu or PM component for deeper review
tool execution, source download, installation, update, removal, Agent launch, Hook enablement, adaptation, admission, or release is proposed
